module: Module 4 - Global Infrastructure, Edge, and Automation

sections.title: AWS Global Expansion Concept
points: AWS allows you to deploy applications globally using Regions and edge locations.
Global expansion requires planning for performance, compliance, and cost.
Applications should be designed for scalability and consistency across locations.
sections.exam_tip: Global users → use multiple Regions + edge services.

sections.title: AWS Regions
points: A Region is a geographic area with multiple data centers.
Each Region is isolated from others.
Data does not leave a Region unless explicitly configured.
Regions help meet compliance and latency requirements.
sections.exam_tip: Data residency or legal requirement → choose correct Region.

sections.title: Availability Zones (AZs)
points: AZs are isolated data centers within a Region.
AZs provide high availability and fault tolerance.
Each AZ has independent power, networking, and connectivity.

sections.title: Edge Locations
points: Edge locations cache content close to users.
Used by services like CloudFront and Route 53.
Reduce latency by serving content locally.

sections.title: Amazon CloudFront
points: CloudFront is a Content Delivery Network (CDN).
Distributes content globally using edge locations.

sections.title: Amazon Route 53
points: Translates domain names into IP addresses.
Routes users to the best endpoint.
Supports health checks and routing policies.

sections.title: Choosing an AWS Region
points: Feature availability (not all services in all Regions).
sections.exam_tip: Order matters: Compliance → Proximity → Features → Cost.

sections.title: Compliance Considerations
points: Some data must stay in specific countries.
Examples include GDPR and financial regulations.
Data sovereignty laws may restrict movement.
sections.exam_tip: Legal requirement ALWAYS overrides everything else.

sections.title: Feature Availability
points: Not all AWS services exist in all Regions.
Special Regions like GovCloud have unique services.

sections.title: Pricing Differences
points: Energy, taxes, and infrastructure costs affect pricing.
Some Regions are cheaper than others.
sections.exam_tip: Same service ≠ same price across Regions.

sections.title: Multi-AZ Architecture
sections.exam_tip: Single AZ = risk. Multi-AZ = best practice.

sections.title: Multi-Region Architecture
sections.exam_tip: Entire Region failure → multi-Region saves you.

sections.title: High Availability
points: System remains operational with minimal downtime.
Achieved using multiple AZs or Regions.

sections.title: Agility
points: Ability to quickly deploy and adapt.

sections.title: AWS SDKs
points: Allow applications to interact with AWS.

sections.title: Access Methods Summary
sections.exam_tip: Know WHEN to use each one.

sections.title: Real Exam Patterns
sections.exam_tip: AWS questions are about choosing the RIGHT tool.

sections.title: Common Exam Traps
points: Multi-AZ is NOT the same as multi-Region.
